   235→  - `validate.ts` — artifact validation rules (file_exists, not_empty,
   236→    contains_section, custom_script, frontmatter_field)
   237→  - `state.ts` — RunState persistence to `state.json`, resume logic,
   238→    phase registry (`setPhaseRegistry()`, `clearPhaseRegistry()`,
   239→    `getPhaseForNode()`), cost aggregation (`updateRunCost()` sums
   240→    `nodes[*].cost_usd` → `total_cost_usd`; called from
   241→    `markNodeCompleted()` when optional `costUsd` param provided, FR-32)
   242→  - `agent.ts` — Claude CLI invocation, continuation loop, retry.
   243→    `AgentRunOptions.model` and `InvokeOptions.model`: optional string for
   244→    per-node model selection. `buildClaudeArgs()` emits `--model <value>` when
   245→    `opts.model` is set AND `opts.resumeSessionId` is NOT set (resume inherits
   246→    original model from session). Resolution: `node.model ?? defaults.model ??
   247→    undefined` (computed in engine.ts/loop.ts, passed as field).
   248→    `executeClaudeProcess()` uses `--output-format stream-json` and reads
   249→    stdout line-by-line. Each JSON line appended to `streamLogPath` file
   250→    (crash-resilient incremental write via `Deno.writeFile({ append: true })`).
   251→    **Stream log timestamps (FR-33):** `tsPrefix()` returns `[HH:MM:SS]`
   252→    wall-clock prefix; `stampLines()` prepends it to each non-empty line (empty
   253→    lines pass through). Applied to log file writes only — terminal output via
   254→    `onOutput` callback receives raw text without timestamps.
   255→    On `result` event: extracts `ClaudeCliOutput` fields (`result`,
   256→    `session_id`, `is_error`, `total_cost_usd`, `duration_ms`,
   257→    `duration_api_ms`, `num_turns`, `permission_denials`). `is_error` derived
   258→    from `subtype !== "success"`. No `result` event → throws descriptive error.
   259→    `streamLogPath` accepted as required parameter in `executeClaudeProcess()`.
   260→    Append semantics: multiple invocations (continuation) with same path
   261→    produce concatenated JSONL. `--verbose` flag removed from
   262→    `buildClaudeArgs()` (unrelated to streaming, changes stderr globally).
   263→    **Repeated file read warning (FR-39):** `FileReadTracker` class in
   264→    `agent.ts`. `track(path): string | null` — maintains `Map<string, number>`,
   265→    returns `[WARN] repeated file read: <path> (<N> times)` when count >
   266→    threshold (default 2), else null. Instantiated per `executeClaudeProcess()`
   267→    call (counter resets per invocation). In event loop: for `tool_use` blocks
   268→    with `name === "Read"`, calls `tracker.track(block.input.file_path)`. Non-
   269→    null result written to `logFile` via `stampLines()`. Log-file-only (terminal
   270→    `onOutput` unchanged). Pure-logic class — unit-testable without I/O.
   271→    **Turn separators and summary footer (FR-40):** `executeClaudeProcess()`
   272→    maintains `turnCount` counter. On each `event.type === "assistant"`:
   273→    increments counter, writes `--- turn N ---` line to `logFile` via
   274→    `stampLines()` (timestamped, consistent with existing log writes). After
   275→    `result` event extraction: writes `--- end ---` + one-line summary via
   276→    `formatFooter(output: ClaudeCliOutput): string`. Footer format:
   277→    `status=<ok|error> duration=<X>s cost=$<Y> turns=<N>`. Both separators and
   278→    footer are log-file-only (terminal `onOutput` callback unchanged).
   279→    `formatFooter()` is a pure function — unit-testable without CLI.
   280→    **Repeated file read warning (FR-40):** `executeClaudeProcess()` maintains
   281→    `readCounts: Map<string, number>` tracking per-path `Read` tool-use events.
   282→    On each `assistant` event: iterates `message.content` blocks, detects
   283→    `tool_use` with `name === "Read"`, extracts `input.file_path`, increments
   284→    count. When count > 2: writes warning to `logFile` via `stampLines()`.
   285→    `checkRepeatedRead(readCounts, filePath): string | null` — helper: increments
   286→    map, returns formatted warning when count > 2, else null.
   287→    `formatRepeatedReadWarning(path, count): string` — pure function returning
   288→    `[WARN] repeated file read: <path> (<N> times)`. Exported for unit testing.
   289→    Warning is log-only (no `onOutput` callback). Counters reset per invocation
   290→    (map is local to `executeClaudeProcess()` call). Execution not blocked.
   291→    **Semi-verbose filtering (FR-41):** `formatEventForOutput(event,
   292→    verbosity?)` accepts optional `Verbosity` param. When
   293→    `verbosity === "semi-verbose"`, skips `tool_use` content blocks in
   294→    `assistant` events — emits only `text` blocks. Default `undefined` =
   295→    all blocks (backward-compatible). Log file writes call without verbosity
   296→    (full output preserved). `onOutput` callback path passes verbosity from
   297→    `AgentRunOptions` so terminal output is filtered at source
   298→  - `loop.ts` — loop node execution with condition extraction, per-iteration
   299→    `AgentResult` accumulation into `LoopResult.bodyResults`.
   300→    `buildLoopBodyOrder()` reads from inline `nodes` sub-object (replaces
   301→    `body` array), topo-sorts body nodes by their `inputs` declarations.
   302→    `buildContext()` resolves `inputs` against both sibling body nodes and
   303→    top-level nodes. Accepts `streamLogPath` pattern from engine; computes
   304→    iteration-qualified path `${nodeId}-iter-${i}.jsonl` per body node
   305→    invocation; forwards to inner `runAgent()` calls
   306→  - `hitl.ts` — HITL detection (`detectHitlRequest`) and poll loop
   307→    (`runHitlLoop`); injectable `scriptRunner`/`claudeRunner` for testing
   308→  - `human.ts` — terminal user input, abort logic
   309→  - ~~`git.ts`~~ — **deleted** (FR-29: domain-specific git code removed from
   310→    engine). Functions relocated to `.sdlc/scripts/rollback-uncommitted.sh`.
   311→    Failure handling replaced by configurable `on_failure_script` hook
   312→  - `output.ts` — terminal output manager (quiet/normal/semi-verbose/verbose),
   313→    verbose methods for detailed agent-node diagnostics.
   314→    `nodeOutput()` gate: shown when `verbosity === "verbose"` or
   315→    `verbosity === "semi-verbose"`. In semi-verbose, tool-call lines already
   316→    excluded upstream by `formatEventForOutput()` — `nodeOutput()` passes
   317→    through whatever it receives.
   318→    `dryRunPlan(levels, labels, postPipelineNodeIds?, runOnMap?)`: renders
   319→    regular DAG levels, then optional "Post-pipeline" section listing `run_on`
   320→    nodes with their conditions (FR-28).
   321→    `nodeResult(nodeId, output: ClaudeCliOutput)`: one-line agent result
   322→    summary (FR-30). Guarded by `verbosity !== "quiet"`. Format:
   323→    `[HH:MM:SS] <nodeId padded>  RESULT: <first line ≤120 chars> | cost=$X.XXXX | duration=Xs | turns=N`.
   324→    Imports `ClaudeCliOutput` from `types.ts`
   325→  - `engine.ts` — main executor: level iteration, parallel dispatch, verbose
   326→    input resolution, node result summary display (FR-30),
   327→    loop-node log saving via `onNodeComplete` callback,
   328→    phase registry init (`setPhaseRegistry()` before `ensureRunDirs()` in both
   329→    fresh and resume paths), phase subdir creation in `ensureRunDirs()`,
   330→    pre-post-pipeline `on_failure_script` execution.
   331→    Dry-run path (FR-28): applies `collectPostPipelineNodes()` +
   332→    `sortPostPipelineNodes()` + level filtering before calling
   333→    `dryRunPlan()`, passing filtered levels and post-pipeline node IDs with
   334→    `run_on` conditions — mirrors normal execution path's filtering logic.
   335→    On config load: iterates all nodes; for loop nodes with `nodes`
   336→    sub-object, flattens nested body node IDs into master ID list passed
   337→    to `createRunState()` (ensures state.json tracks both top-level and
   338→    nested body node IDs).
   339→    Computes `streamLogPath = ${runDir}/logs/${nodeId}.jsonl` for each agent
   340→    node; passes to `runAgent()`. For loop nodes: passes path pattern to
   341→    loop executor for iteration-qualified derivation
   342→  - `cli.ts` — CLI entry point: argument parsing, .env loading
   343→  - `mod.ts` — public API re-exports
   344→- **Interfaces:**
   345→  - CLI: `deno task run [--prompt <text>] [--config <path>] [--resume <run-id>]
   346→    [--dry-run] [-v|-s|-q] [--env KEY=VAL] [--skip nodes] [--only nodes]`
   347→  - Config: `.sdlc/pipeline.yaml` (YAML, version "1")
   348→  - State: `.sdlc/runs/<run-id>/state.json` (JSON)
   349→- **Node types:** `agent`, `merge`, `loop` (with inline `nodes` sub-object
   350→    for body node definitions), `human`
   351→- **Node flags:**
   352→  - `run_on?: "always" | "success" | "failure"` — execution condition for
   353→    post-pipeline nodes. When set, node is excluded from DAG levels and executes
   354→    in a post-pipeline step after all DAG levels complete:
   355→    - `"always"` — execute regardless of pipeline outcome. Used for meta-agent
   356→      and tech-lead-review.
   357→    - `"success"` — execute only if pipeline succeeded.
   358→    - `"failure"` — execute only if pipeline failed. Skipped nodes get
   359→      `markNodeSkipped()` status.
   360→    Backward compat: `run_always: true` in YAML normalized to `run_on: "always"`
   361→    by config loader (see `config.ts` normalization). `run_always` deleted
   362→    post-normalization — not visible to engine runtime.
   363→  - `phase?: string` — optional phase grouping label (e.g., `plan`, `impl`,
   364→    `report`). When set, node artifacts are stored under
   365→    `<run-dir>/<phase>/<node-id>/` instead of `<run-dir>/<node-id>/`. User-
   366→    defined (no enum constraint). Validated: must be non-empty string if present.
   367→    Backward-compatible: omitting `phase` preserves flat layout.
   368→  - `env?: Record<string, string>` — optional node-level environment variables.
   369→    Merged with global env (node-level overrides global defaults). Accessible
   370→    in template context via `{{env.<key>}}`.
   371→  - `model?: string` — per-node Claude model override (FR-27, implemented).
   372→    Overrides `defaults.model`. Absent = defaults.model or CLI default (no flag).
   373→    Emitted as `--model <value>` on initial invocations only; `--resume` calls
   374→    exclude `--model` (session inherits original). Resolution chain:
   375→    `node.model ?? defaults.model ?? undefined`. Centralized in
   376→    `buildClaudeArgs()` via `InvokeOptions.model` field.
   377→- **Commit strategy:** Engine does not auto-commit. Developer agent owns commits
   378→  (`git add`, `git commit`, `git push` per task). No dedicated committer nodes.
   379→- **Verbose Output (Direct Injection pattern):**
   380→  - `output.ts` exposes 4 verbose-guarded methods on `OutputManager`:
   381→    `verbosePrompt(nodeId, prompt)`,
   382→    `verboseInputs(nodeId, inputs: {path, sizeBytes}[])`,
   383→    `verboseValidation(nodeId, results: {rule, passed, detail?}[])`,
   384→    `verboseContinuation(nodeId, attempt, max, failures)`.
   385→    `verboseSafety()` and `verboseCommit()` removed (engine no longer performs
   386→    safety checks or commits — FR-29 domain-agnostic cleanup).
   387→    All no-op when `verbosity !== "verbose"`. Output: human-readable stderr with
   388→    section headers. Note: AC #5 (agent stdout streaming) already implemented
   389→    via existing `nodeOutput()` method — no new work needed.
   390→  - `agent.ts`: `AgentRunOptions` gains optional `output?: OutputManager` and
   391→    `nodeId?: string`. `runAgent()` calls `verbosePrompt()` after prompt
   392→    construction, `verboseValidation()` after each `runValidations()` call,
   393→    `verboseContinuation()` before resume invocation. Guarded by `if (output)`.
   394→  - `loop.ts`: `LoopRunOptions` gains optional `output?: OutputManager`.
   395→    Forwarded to `runAgent()` calls. Enables prompt/validation/continuation
   396→    verbose for loop body nodes. Safety/commit verbose for loop body nodes:
   397→    deferred (loop body bypasses `executeAgentNode()`).
   398→  - `git.ts`: **Deleted** (FR-29). All git functions removed from engine.
   399→    Failure rollback replaced by `on_failure_script` hook (FR-34).
   400→    `CommitResult` type removed. Safety check and commit verbose methods
   401→    removed from `output.ts`.
   402→  - `engine.ts`: `executeAgentNode()` resolves input artifact paths+sizes by
   403→    walking `ctx.input` directories via `Deno.stat()`; calls
   404→    `this.output.verboseInputs()` before `runAgent()`. Passes `this.output`
   405→    and `nodeId` to `runAgent()`. Safety check and commit verbose removed
   406→    (engine no longer performs these — FR-29). `runFailureHook(script?)`:
   407→    private method (~10 lines), executes `on_failure_script` via
   408→    `Deno.Command()` on pipeline failure. Swallows errors (failure hook must
   409→    not crash engine). Replaces hard-wired `rollbackUncommitted()`.
   410→  - All existing callers pass no `output` arg — zero behavioral change.
   411→- **Deps:** `claude` CLI, `deno`, `git`, `jsr:@std/yaml`.
   412→
   413→### 3.7 Phase Registry (`state.ts`)
   414→
   415→- **Purpose:** Module-scoped mapping from nodeId → phase string, enabling
   416→  `getNodeDir()` to resolve phase-aware artifact paths without signature change.
   417→- **Data:** `phaseRegistry: Map<string, string>` — populated from
   418→  `PipelineConfig` nodes' `phase` fields.
   419→- **Interfaces:**
   420→  - `setPhaseRegistry(config: PipelineConfig)` — iterates config nodes, builds
   421→    map from `nodeId → node.phase` (skips nodes without `phase`). Called once at
   422→    engine init (both fresh-run and `--resume` paths).
   423→  - `clearPhaseRegistry()` — resets map. Used in tests for isolation.
   424→  - `getPhaseForNode(nodeId: string): string | undefined` — lookup.
   425→  - `getNodeDir(runId, nodeId)` — signature unchanged. Internally: if registry
   426→    has phase for nodeId, returns `${runDir}/${phase}/${nodeId}/`; otherwise
   427→    `${runDir}/${nodeId}/` (backward-compatible fallback).
   428→- **Deps:** `types.ts` (`PipelineConfig`, `NodeConfig`).
   429→- **Design rationale:** Module-scoped global state (not instance state) because
   430→  `getNodeDir()` is a free function called from multiple contexts (engine,
   431→  templates, tests). Single-instance engine guarantee prevents concurrent
   432→  mutation. `clearPhaseRegistry()` ensures test isolation.
   433→
   434→### 3.8 HITL Pipeline Scripts (`.sdlc/scripts/hitl-*.sh`)
   435→
   436→- **Purpose:** Deliver agent questions to humans and poll for replies. Pipeline-
   437→  specific (GitHub), not engine code. Engine invokes via configurable paths.
   438→- **Scripts:**
   439→  - `hitl-ask.sh` — render question JSON → markdown, post to GitHub issue.
   440→    - Input: `--run-dir`, `--artifact-source`, `--run-id`, `--node-id`,
   441→      `--question-json`.
   442→    - Extracts issue: `yq '.issue' "$RUN_DIR/$ISSUE_SOURCE"`.
   443→    - Auto-detects repo: `gh repo view --json nameWithOwner`.
   444→    - Renders: header, blockquoted question, numbered options, HTML marker
   445→      `<!-- hitl:<run-id>:<node-id> -->`.
   446→    - Posts via `gh issue comment <N> --body "$md"`.
   447→    - Deps: `jq`, `yq`, `gh`.
   448→  - `hitl-check.sh` — poll GitHub issue for human reply after marker.
   449→    - Input: `--run-dir`, `--artifact-source`, `--run-id`, `--node-id`,
   450→      `--exclude-login`.
   451→    - Extracts issue: `yq '.issue' "$RUN_DIR/$ISSUE_SOURCE"`.
   452→    - Auto-detects repo: `gh repo view --json nameWithOwner`.
   453→    - Fetches comments: `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/<N>/comments`.
   454→    - jq filter: find comment with marker, then first subsequent non-bot comment.
   455→    - Exit 0 + body on stdout = reply found. Exit 1 = no reply yet.
   456→    - Deps: `jq`, `yq`, `gh`.
   457→- **Interfaces:** Called by engine via `defaults.hitl.ask_script` /
   458→  `defaults.hitl.check_script` paths in `pipeline.yaml`.
   459→
   460→### 3.9 Pipeline Trigger
   461→
   462→- **Purpose:** Single entry point for pipeline. PM agent autonomously triages
   463→  open GitHub issues.
   464→- **Interfaces:** CLI: `deno task run [--prompt "..."]`. PM selects
   465→  highest-priority open issue via `gh`.
   466→- **Deps:** Devcontainer, Claude CLI auth (OAuth or API key), `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
   467→
   468→### 3.10 Dashboard Generator (`scripts/generate-dashboard.ts`) (FR-33, FR-35, FR-38, FR-40, issue #15)
   469→
   470→- **Purpose:** Generate self-contained HTML dashboard summarizing pipeline run
   471→  results. Reads `state.json` + per-node `logs/*.json`. Produces `index.html`
   472→  in run directory with all CSS inlined (no CDN deps).
   473→- **Functions:**
   474→  - `readRunState(runDir)` — parse `state.json` → `RunState`
   475→  - `readNodeLog(runDir, nodeId)` — parse `logs/<nodeId>.json` →
   476→    `ClaudeCliOutput`
   477→  - `renderCard(nodeId, state, log, streamLogHref?)` — HTML card: status badge,
   478→    timing, cost, result summary via `<details><summary>` (first 3 lines
   479→    preview, full text in details body). Single-line results render without
   480→    `<details>` wrapper. When `streamLogHref` provided: renders
   481→    `<a class="log-link" href="${escHtml(streamLogHref)}">stream log</a>` after
   482→    card-meta div. Omitted when absent (backward-compatible).
   483→  - `renderHtml(runDir, state, logs, streamLogHrefs?)` — full page: run metadata
   484→    header, phase-grouped card grid, inlined CSS. 4th param
   485→    `streamLogHrefs?: Record<string, string>` maps nodeId → relative href;
   486→    threaded to each `renderCard()` call via lookup
   487→  - `escHtml(str)` — escape `<>&"'` for XSS-safe HTML embedding
   488→  - `computeTimeline(state: RunState)` — iterates `state.nodes`, parses
   489→    `started_at` ISO timestamps, computes `offsetPct`/`widthPct` relative to
   490→    run start/total duration. Identifies bottleneck (max `duration_ms`). Omits
   491→    nodes with missing timing. Returns `{nodeId, offsetPct, widthPct,
   492→    durationMs, isBottleneck}[]`
   493→  - `renderTimeline(bars)` — generates Gantt-style HTML timeline section:
   494→    container with relative positioning, bars absolutely-positioned per row
   495→    (sorted by `started_at`). Bottleneck bar gets `.timeline-bottleneck` CSS
   496→    class. Labels sanitized via `escHtml()`. Timeline CSS appended to existing
   497→    `CSS` const (inlined, no CDN deps). Integrated into `renderHtml()` between
   498→    header and card grid (FR-38)
   499→- **Stream log link flow (issue #15):** CLI entry point scans each node
   500→  directory for `stream.log` existence via `Deno.stat()`. For nodes with phases,
   501→  computes relative path as `<phase>/<nodeId>/stream.log`; without phase:
   502→  `<nodeId>/stream.log`. Builds `Record<string, string>` href map, passes to
   503→  `renderHtml()` → threaded to `renderCard()`. CSS: `.log-link` class (monospace,
   504→  smaller font, muted color — distinct from result text).
   505→- **Functions (continued):**
   506→  - `computeCostBars(state: RunState)` — filters `state.nodes` by
   507→    `cost_usd > 0`, computes proportional `widthPct` relative to max cost.
   508→    Returns `{nodeId: string, costUsd: number, widthPct: number}[]` (FR-40)
   509→  - `renderCostChart(bars, totalCost)` — inline SVG horizontal bar chart.
   510→    Each bar: `<rect>` with proportional width, `<text>` label (node ID via
   511→    `escHtml()`), cost value annotation. Total cost header. Empty bars →
   512→    "No cost data" message (mirrors timeline empty-state). Cost chart CSS
   513→    appended to `CSS` const. Integrated into `renderHtml()` between timeline
   514→    and `<main>` card grid (FR-40)
   515→- **Interfaces:**
   516→  - CLI: `deno task dashboard --run-dir <path>`
   517→  - Hook: `after:` on `optimize` node (`|| true` suffix for non-fatal)
   518→- **Deps:** `engine/types.ts` (imports `RunState`, `ClaudeCliOutput` types
   519→  for parsing). No runtime engine dependency — reads JSON files directly.
   520→
   521→## 4. Data
   522→
   523→- **Entities:**
   524→  - Handoff Artifact: Structured Markdown (01-spec.md through 07-changelog.md)
   525→  - Agent Log: Claude CLI JSON output (`.sdlc/runs/<run-id>/logs/<node-id>.json`)
   526→  - Agent Prompt: SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (`.claude/skills/agent-<name>/SKILL.md`)
   527→  - Run State: JSON (`.sdlc/runs/<run-id>/state.json`)
   528→  - Pipeline Config: YAML (`.sdlc/pipeline.yaml`). Top-level keys: `name`,
   529→    `version`, `defaults`, `phases` (FR-33), `nodes`. `phases` key declares
   530→    named phase groups with member stage IDs (e.g., `plan: [specification,
   531→    design, decision]`). Engine treats `phases` as opaque config data.
   532→    Node IDs use activity-based naming (FR-33): `specification`, `design`,
   533→    `decision`, `implementation`, `build`, `verify`, `tech-lead-review`, `optimize`
   534→  - ~~CommitResult~~: **Deleted** (FR-29: engine no longer commits)
   535→  - ValidationRule: `{ type: "file_exists"|"file_not_empty"|"contains_section"|
   536→    "custom_script"|"frontmatter_field", path?, field?, allowed?, ... }`
   537→  - LoopResult: `{ ..., bodyResults: AgentResult[] }` — accumulated per-iteration
   538→    agent results; consumed by `executeLoopNode()` callback for log saving
   539→  - LoopNodeConfig: `{ ..., nodes: Record<string, NodeConfig> }` — inline
   540→    body node definitions replacing `body: string[]`. Each key is a body
   541→    node ID, value is its full node config. `condition_node` must reference
   542→    a key in `nodes`. Body node ordering derived from `inputs` declarations
   543→    via topo-sort (>1 entry requires at least one `inputs` reference to
   544→    prevent disconnected graph with arbitrary order).
   545→  - NodeState: `{ ..., cost_usd?: number }` — per-node cost from
   546→    `ClaudeCliOutput.total_cost_usd`, set at completion via
   547→    `markNodeCompleted()` optional param (FR-32)
   548→  - RunState: `{ ..., total_cost_usd?: number }` — sum of all
   549→    `nodes[*].cost_usd`, recomputed by `updateRunCost()` on each node
   550→    completion (FR-32)
   551→  - NodeConfig: `{ ..., run_on?: "always"|"success"|"failure", phase?: string,
   552→    env?: Record<string, string>, model?: string }` — `run_on` for conditional
   553→    post-pipeline execution; `phase` for artifact directory grouping; `env` for
   554→    node-level env vars; `model` for per-node Claude model override (FR-27)
   555→- **ERD:** N/A (file-based, no database).
   556→- **Migration:** N/A.
   557→
   558→### 4.1 Inter-Node Data Flow
   559→
   560→- **Mechanism:** Filesystem-based. Each node reads input via `{{input.<node-id>}}`
   561→  template variable pointing to predecessor's output directory. No manifest.
   562→- **Directory structure:** `.sdlc/runs/<run-id>/[<phase>/]<node-id>/` per node
   563→  output. Phase subdir present when node's `phase` field is set in config.
   564→  Example with phases: `.sdlc/runs/abc/plan/specification/`, `.sdlc/runs/abc/impl/build/`.
   565→  Without phase: `.sdlc/runs/abc/some-node/` (backward-compatible flat layout).
   566→- **Validation:** Engine validates output via configurable rules (file_exists,
   567→  file_not_empty, contains_section, custom_script, frontmatter_field) after
   568→  each node. Validation failures trigger continuation (resume with error
   569→  context) rather than immediate node failure.
   570→  - `frontmatter_field`: Reads artifact file, extracts YAML frontmatter via
   571→    `^---\n([\s\S]*?)\n---` regex, parses target field, checks value against
   572→    allowed set. Config: `{ type: "frontmatter_field", path, field, allowed }`.
   573→  - `contains_section`: Checks artifact file for presence of a markdown section.
   574→    Supports `on_error: continue` (non-fatal). Used by meta-agent for
   575→    "Fixes Applied" section validation.
   576→  - Developer node uses `custom_script` validation rule (not `after` hook) for
   577→    `deno task check`, enabling continuation-on-failure for check errors.
   578→- **Context management:** Claude CLI auto-compression handles large input sets.
   579→- **Template variables:** `{{node_dir}}`, `{{input.*}}`, `{{run_dir}}`,
   580→  `{{run_id}}`, `{{args.*}}`, `{{env.*}}`, `{{loop.iteration}}`.
   581→- **After-hook conventions:** Commands run from repo root (no `cd {{run_dir}}`
   582→  prefix needed). Use `|| true` suffix to prevent hook failure from killing
   583→  the node. Example (sds-update diff capture):
   584→  `git diff HEAD -- documents/design.md > {{node_dir}}/04a-sds-diff.md;
   585→  [ -s {{node_dir}}/04a-sds-diff.md ] || echo "No changes" >
   586→  {{node_dir}}/04a-sds-diff.md || true`.
   587→
   588→### 4.2 Commit Strategy
   589→
   590→- **Branch:** Feature branch created by tech-lead agent (`git checkout -b
   591→  sdlc/issue-<N>`). Fallback for `--prompt` mode: `sdlc/{{run_id}}`.
   592→- **Commit cadence (FR-26):** Developer-owned commits. No dedicated committer
   593→  agent nodes. Developer runs `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` after each
   594→  task. Commit messages follow `sdlc(impl): <summary>` format.
   595→- **PR creation:** Tech-lead creates draft PR (`gh pr create --draft`) before
   596→  impl-loop. Developer pushes to same branch. QA posts PR review verdicts.
   597→- **Post-pipeline:** Tech-lead-review performs final review + CI gate + merge.
   598→- **Engine invariant:** Engine does NOT auto-commit (FR-14 preserved). All git
   599→  operations happen inside agent prompts.

file.content:
- **Deps:** None (static content, versioned in git). ### 3.6 Pipeline Engine (`engine/`) - **Purpose:** Configurable DAG-based pipeline executor. Replaces hardcoded shell script orchestration with YAML-driven node graph. - **Modules:** - `types.ts` — type declarations (incl. `ValidationRule.type` union, `NodeConfig.run_on` (`"always"|"success"|"failure"`), `NodeConfig.phase`, `NodeConfig.env`, `NodeConfig.model` (per-node Claude model override), `PipelineDefaults.model` (default model for all nodes), `LoopNodeConfig.nodes` (inline body node definitions), `LoopResult.bodyResults`, `ErrorCategory` (structured failure enum), `NodeState.error_category`, `NodeState.cost_usd` (FR-32 per-node cost), `RunState.total_cost_usd` (FR-32 aggregated run cost), `PipelineDefaults.on_failure_script` (FR-34 configurable failure hook), `HitlConfig.artifact_source` (renamed from `issue_source`), `HitlConfig.exclude_login` (renamed from `bot_login`), `Verbosity` union: `"quiet"|"normal"|"semi-verbose"|"verbose"` (FR-41)) - `template.ts` — `{{var}}` interpolation for prompts/paths - `config.ts` — YAML parsing, schema validation, defaults merge, `run_on` normalization. `validateNode()`: if `run_on` present, must be one of `"always"|"success"|"failure"`; error: `Node '' has invalid run_on value ''. Must be one of: always, success, failure`. `normalizeRunOn()` pass (in `mergeDefaults()`): if `node.run_always === true && !node.run_on` → sets `run_on = "always"`; if both present, `run_on` wins; deletes `run_always` from config post-normalization (downstream code only sees `run_on`). Loop nodes: parses `nodes` sub-object, validates body node ordering (>1 entry requires `inputs` declarations), validates `condition_node` references valid key in `nodes`. Skips top-level existence check for body node IDs referenced in `inputs`. - `dag.ts` — topological sort, cycle detection, level grouping. Excludes loop body nodes (from `nodes` sub-object) from top-level graph; loop node itself remains in DAG with its declared `inputs`. - `validate.ts` — artifact validation rules (file_exists, not_empty, contains_section, custom_script, frontmatter_field) - `state.ts` — RunState persistence to `state.json`, resume logic, phase registry (`setPhaseRegistry()`, `clearPhaseRegistry()`, `getPhaseForNode()`), cost aggregation (`updateRunCost()` sums `nodes[*].cost_usd` → `total_cost_usd`; called from `markNodeCompleted()` when optional `costUsd` param provided, FR-32) - `agent.ts` — Claude CLI invocation, continuation loop, retry. `AgentRunOptions.model` and `InvokeOptions.model`: optional string for per-node model selection. `buildClaudeArgs()` emits `--model ` when `opts.model` is set AND `opts.resumeSessionId` is NOT set (resume inherits original model from session). Resolution: `node.model ?? defaults.model ?? undefined` (computed in engine.ts/loop.ts, passed as field). `executeClaudeProcess()` uses `--output-format stream-json` and reads stdout line-by-line. Each JSON line appended to `streamLogPath` file (crash-resilient incremental write via `Deno.writeFile({ append: true })`). **Stream log timestamps (FR-33):** `tsPrefix()` returns `[HH:MM:SS]` wall-clock prefix; `stampLines()` prepends it to each non-empty line (empty lines pass through). Applied to log file writes only — terminal output via `onOutput` callback receives raw text without timestamps. On `result` event: extracts `ClaudeCliOutput` fields (`result`, `session_id`, `is_error`, `total_cost_usd`, `duration_ms`, `duration_api_ms`, `num_turns`, `permission_denials`). `is_error` derived from `subtype !== "success"`. No `result` event → throws descriptive error. `streamLogPath` accepted as required parameter in `executeClaudeProcess()`. Append semantics: multiple invocations (continuation) with same path produce concatenated JSONL. `--verbose` flag removed from `buildClaudeArgs()` (unrelated to streaming, changes stderr globally). **Repeated file read warning (FR-39):** `FileReadTracker` class in `agent.ts`. `track(path): string | null` — maintains `Map`, returns `[WARN] repeated file read: ( times)` when count > threshold (default 2), else null. Instantiated per `executeClaudeProcess()` call (counter resets per invocation). In event loop: for `tool_use` blocks with `name === "Read"`, calls `tracker.track(block.input.file_path)`. Non- null result written to `logFile` via `stampLines()`. Log-file-only (terminal `onOutput` unchanged). Pure-logic class — unit-testable without I/O. **Turn separators and summary footer (FR-40):** `executeClaudeProcess()` maintains `turnCount` counter. On each `event.type === "assistant"`: increments counter, writes `--- turn N ---` line to `logFile` via `stampLines()` (timestamped, consistent with existing log writes). After `result` event extraction: writes `--- end ---` + one-line summary via `formatFooter(output: ClaudeCliOutput): string`. Footer format: `status= duration=s cost=$ turns=`. Both separators and footer are log-file-only (terminal `onOutput` callback unchanged). `formatFooter()` is a pure function — unit-testable without CLI. **Repeated file read warning (FR-40):** `executeClaudeProcess()` maintains `readCounts: Map` tracking per-path `Read` tool-use events. On each `assistant` event: iterates `message.content` blocks, detects `tool_use` with `name === "Read"`, extracts `input.file_path`, increments count. When count > 2: writes warning to `logFile` via `stampLines()`. `checkRepeatedRead(readCounts, filePath): string | null` — helper: increments map, returns formatted warning when count > 2, else null. `formatRepeatedReadWarning(path, count): string` — pure function returning `[WARN] repeated file read: ( times)`. Exported for unit testing. Warning is log-only (no `onOutput` callback). Counters reset per invocation (map is local to `executeClaudeProcess()` call). Execution not blocked. **Semi-verbose filtering (FR-41):** `formatEventForOutput(event, verbosity?)` accepts optional `Verbosity` param. When `verbosity === "semi-verbose"`, skips `tool_use` content blocks in `assistant` events — emits only `text` blocks. Default `undefined` = all blocks (backward-compatible). Log file writes call without verbosity (full output preserved). `onOutput` callback path passes verbosity from `AgentRunOptions` so terminal output is filtered at source - `loop.ts` — loop node execution with condition extraction, per-iteration `AgentResult` accumulation into `LoopResult.bodyResults`. `buildLoopBodyOrder()` reads from inline `nodes` sub-object (replaces `body` array), topo-sorts body nodes by their `inputs` declarations. `buildContext()` resolves `inputs` against both sibling body nodes and top-level nodes. Accepts `streamLogPath` pattern from engine; computes iteration-qualified path `${nodeId}-iter-${i}.jsonl` per body node invocation; forwards to inner `runAgent()` calls - `hitl.ts` — HITL detection (`detectHitlRequest`) and poll loop (`runHitlLoop`); injectable `scriptRunner`/`claudeRunner` for testing - `human.ts` — terminal user input, abort logic - ~~`git.ts`~~ — **deleted** (FR-29: domain-specific git code removed from engine). Functions relocated to `.sdlc/scripts/rollback-uncommitted.sh`. Failure handling replaced by configurable `on_failure_script` hook - `output.ts` — terminal output manager (quiet/normal/semi-verbose/verbose), verbose methods for detailed agent-node diagnostics. `nodeOutput()` gate: shown when `verbosity === "verbose"` or `verbosity === "semi-verbose"`. In semi-verbose, tool-call lines already excluded upstream by `formatEventForOutput()` — `nodeOutput()` passes through whatever it receives. `dryRunPlan(levels, labels, postPipelineNodeIds?, runOnMap?)`: renders regular DAG levels, then optional "Post-pipeline" section listing `run_on` nodes with their conditions (FR-28). `nodeResult(nodeId, output: ClaudeCliOutput)`: one-line agent result summary (FR-30). Guarded by `verbosity !== "quiet"`. Format: `[HH:MM:SS] RESULT: | cost=$X.XXXX | duration=Xs | turns=N`. Imports `ClaudeCliOutput` from `types.ts` - `engine.ts` — main executor: level iteration, parallel dispatch, verbose input resolution, node result summary display (FR-30), loop-node log saving via `onNodeComplete` callback, phase registry init (`setPhaseRegistry()` before `ensureRunDirs()` in both fresh and resume paths), phase subdir creation in `ensureRunDirs()`, pre-post-pipeline `on_failure_script` execution. Dry-run path (FR-28): applies `collectPostPipelineNodes()` + `sortPostPipelineNodes()` + level filtering before calling `dryRunPlan()`, passing filtered levels and post-pipeline node IDs with `run_on` conditions — mirrors normal execution path's filtering logic. On config load: iterates all nodes; for loop nodes with `nodes` sub-object, flattens nested body node IDs into master ID list passed to `createRunState()` (ensures state.json tracks both top-level and nested body node IDs). Computes `streamLogPath = ${runDir}/logs/${nodeId}.jsonl` for each agent node; passes to `runAgent()`. For loop nodes: passes path pattern to loop executor for iteration-qualified derivation - `cli.ts` — CLI entry point: argument parsing, .env loading - `mod.ts` — public API re-exports - **Interfaces:** - CLI: `deno task run [--prompt ] [--config ] [--resume ] [--dry-run] [-v|-s|-q] [--env KEY=VAL] [--skip nodes] [--only nodes]` - Config: `.sdlc/pipeline.yaml` (YAML, version "1") - State: `.sdlc/runs//state.json` (JSON) - **Node types:** `agent`, `merge`, `loop` (with inline `nodes` sub-object for body node definitions), `human` - **Node flags:** - `run_on?: "always" | "success" | "failure"` — execution condition for post-pipeline nodes. When set, node is excluded from DAG levels and executes in a post-pipeline step after all DAG levels complete: - `"always"` — execute regardless of pipeline outcome. Used for meta-agent and tech-lead-review. - `"success"` — execute only if pipeline succeeded. - `"failure"` — execute only if pipeline failed. Skipped nodes get `markNodeSkipped()` status. Backward compat: `run_always: true` in YAML normalized to `run_on: "always"` by config loader (see `config.ts` normalization). `run_always` deleted post-normalization — not visible to engine runtime. - `phase?: string` — optional phase grouping label (e.g., `plan`, `impl`, `report`). When set, node artifacts are stored under `///` instead of `//`. User- defined (no enum constraint). Validated: must be non-empty string if present. Backward-compatible: omitting `phase` preserves flat layout. - `env?: Record` — optional node-level environment variables. Merged with global env (node-level overrides global defaults). Accessible in template context via `{{env.}}`. - `model?: string` — per-node Claude model override (FR-27, implemented). Overrides `defaults.model`. Absent = defaults.model or CLI default (no flag). Emitted as `--model ` on initial invocations only; `--resume` calls exclude `--model` (session inherits original). Resolution chain: `node.model ?? defaults.model ?? undefined`. Centralized in `buildClaudeArgs()` via `InvokeOptions.model` field. - **Commit strategy:** Engine does not auto-commit. Developer agent owns commits (`git add`, `git commit`, `git push` per task). No dedicated committer nodes. - **Verbose Output (Direct Injection pattern):** - `output.ts` exposes 4 verbose-guarded methods on `OutputManager`: `verbosePrompt(nodeId, prompt)`, `verboseInputs(nodeId, inputs: {path, sizeBytes}[])`, `verboseValidation(nodeId, results: {rule, passed, detail?}[])`, `verboseContinuation(nodeId, attempt, max, failures)`. `verboseSafety()` and `verboseCommit()` removed (engine no longer performs safety checks or commits — FR-29 domain-agnostic cleanup). All no-op when `verbosity !== "verbose"`. Output: human-readable stderr with section headers. Note: AC #5 (agent stdout streaming) already implemented via existing `nodeOutput()` method — no new work needed. - `agent.ts`: `AgentRunOptions` gains optional `output?: OutputManager` and `nodeId?: string`. `runAgent()` calls `verbosePrompt()` after prompt construction, `verboseValidation()` after each `runValidations()` call, `verboseContinuation()` before resume invocation. Guarded by `if (output)`. - `loop.ts`: `LoopRunOptions` gains optional `output?: OutputManager`. Forwarded to `runAgent()` calls. Enables prompt/validation/continuation verbose for loop body nodes. Safety/commit verbose for loop body nodes: deferred (loop body bypasses `executeAgentNode()`). - `git.ts`: **Deleted** (FR-29). All git functions removed from engine. Failure rollback replaced by `on_failure_script` hook (FR-34). `CommitResult` type removed. Safety check and commit verbose methods removed from `output.ts`. - `engine.ts`: `executeAgentNode()` resolves input artifact paths+sizes by walking `ctx.input` directories via `Deno.stat()`; calls `this.output.verboseInputs()` before `runAgent()`. Passes `this.output` and `nodeId` to `runAgent()`. Safety check and commit verbose removed (engine no longer performs these — FR-29). `runFailureHook(script?)`: private method (~10 lines), executes `on_failure_script` via `Deno.Command()` on pipeline failure. Swallows errors (failure hook must not crash engine). Replaces hard-wired `rollbackUncommitted()`. - All existing callers pass no `output` arg — zero behavioral change. - **Deps:** `claude` CLI, `deno`, `git`, `jsr:@std/yaml`. ### 3.7 Phase Registry (`state.ts`) - **Purpose:** Module-scoped mapping from nodeId → phase string, enabling `getNodeDir()` to resolve phase-aware artifact paths without signature change. - **Data:** `phaseRegistry: Map` — populated from `PipelineConfig` nodes' `phase` fields. - **Interfaces:** - `setPhaseRegistry(config: PipelineConfig)` — iterates config nodes, builds map from `nodeId → node.phase` (skips nodes without `phase`). Called once at engine init (both fresh-run and `--resume` paths). - `clearPhaseRegistry()` — resets map. Used in tests for isolation. - `getPhaseForNode(nodeId: string): string | undefined` — lookup. - `getNodeDir(runId, nodeId)` — signature unchanged. Internally: if registry has phase for nodeId, returns `${runDir}/${phase}/${nodeId}/`; otherwise `${runDir}/${nodeId}/` (backward-compatible fallback). - **Deps:** `types.ts` (`PipelineConfig`, `NodeConfig`). - **Design rationale:** Module-scoped global state (not instance state) because `getNodeDir()` is a free function called from multiple contexts (engine, templates, tests). Single-instance engine guarantee prevents concurrent mutation. `clearPhaseRegistry()` ensures test isolation. ### 3.8 HITL Pipeline Scripts (`.sdlc/scripts/hitl-*.sh`) - **Purpose:** Deliver agent questions to humans and poll for replies. Pipeline- specific (GitHub), not engine code. Engine invokes via configurable paths. - **Scripts:** - `hitl-ask.sh` — render question JSON → markdown, post to GitHub issue. - Input: `--run-dir`, `--artifact-source`, `--run-id`, `--node-id`, `--question-json`. - Extracts issue: `yq '.issue' "$RUN_DIR/$ISSUE_SOURCE"`. - Auto-detects repo: `gh repo view --json nameWithOwner`. - Renders: header, blockquoted question, numbered options, HTML marker ``. - Posts via `gh issue comment --body "$md"`. - Deps: `jq`, `yq`, `gh`. - `hitl-check.sh` — poll GitHub issue for human reply after marker. - Input: `--run-dir`, `--artifact-source`, `--run-id`, `--node-id`, `--exclude-login`. - Extracts issue: `yq '.issue' "$RUN_DIR/$ISSUE_SOURCE"`. - Auto-detects repo: `gh repo view --json nameWithOwner`. - Fetches comments: `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues//comments`. - jq filter: find comment with marker, then first subsequent non-bot comment. - Exit 0 + body on stdout = reply found. Exit 1 = no reply yet. - Deps: `jq`, `yq`, `gh`. - **Interfaces:** Called by engine via `defaults.hitl.ask_script` / `defaults.hitl.check_script` paths in `pipeline.yaml`. ### 3.9 Pipeline Trigger - **Purpose:** Single entry point for pipeline. PM agent autonomously triages open GitHub issues. - **Interfaces:** CLI: `deno task run [--prompt "..."]`. PM selects highest-priority open issue via `gh`. - **Deps:** Devcontainer, Claude CLI auth (OAuth or API key), `GITHUB_TOKEN`. ### 3.10 Dashboard Generator (`scripts/generate-dashboard.ts`) (FR-33, FR-35, FR-38, FR-40, issue #15) - **Purpose:** Generate self-contained HTML dashboard summarizing pipeline run results. Reads `state.json` + per-node `logs/*.json`. Produces `index.html` in run directory with all CSS inlined (no CDN deps). - **Functions:** - `readRunState(runDir)` — parse `state.json` → `RunState` - `readNodeLog(runDir, nodeId)` — parse `logs/.json` → `ClaudeCliOutput` - `renderCard(nodeId, state, log, streamLogHref?)` — HTML card: status badge, timing, cost, result summary via `
` (first 3 lines preview, full text in details body). Single-line results render without `
` wrapper. When `streamLogHref` provided: renders `stream log` after card-meta div. Omitted when absent (backward-compatible). - `renderHtml(runDir, state, logs, streamLogHrefs?)` — full page: run metadata header, phase-grouped card grid, inlined CSS. 4th param `streamLogHrefs?: Record` maps nodeId → relative href; threaded to each `renderCard()` call via lookup - `escHtml(str)` — escape `<>&"'` for XSS-safe HTML embedding - `computeTimeline(state: RunState)` — iterates `state.nodes`, parses `started_at` ISO timestamps, computes `offsetPct`/`widthPct` relative to run start/total duration. Identifies bottleneck (max `duration_ms`). Omits nodes with missing timing. Returns `{nodeId, offsetPct, widthPct, durationMs, isBottleneck}[]` - `renderTimeline(bars)` — generates Gantt-style HTML timeline section: container with relative positioning, bars absolutely-positioned per row (sorted by `started_at`). Bottleneck bar gets `.timeline-bottleneck` CSS class. Labels sanitized via `escHtml()`. Timeline CSS appended to existing `CSS` const (inlined, no CDN deps). Integrated into `renderHtml()` between header and card grid (FR-38) - **Stream log link flow (issue #15):** CLI entry point scans each node directory for `stream.log` existence via `Deno.stat()`. For nodes with phases, computes relative path as `//stream.log`; without phase: `/stream.log`. Builds `Record` href map, passes to `renderHtml()` → threaded to `renderCard()`. CSS: `.log-link` class (monospace, smaller font, muted color — distinct from result text). - **Functions (continued):** - `computeCostBars(state: RunState)` — filters `state.nodes` by `cost_usd > 0`, computes proportional `widthPct` relative to max cost. Returns `{nodeId: string, costUsd: number, widthPct: number}[]` (FR-40) - `renderCostChart(bars, totalCost)` — inline SVG horizontal bar chart. Each bar: `` with proportional width, `` label (node ID via `escHtml()`), cost value annotation. Total cost header. Empty bars → "No cost data" message (mirrors timeline empty-state). Cost chart CSS appended to `CSS` const. Integrated into `renderHtml()` between timeline and `
` card grid (FR-40) - **Interfaces:** - CLI: `deno task dashboard --run-dir ` - Hook: `after:` on `optimize` node (`|| true` suffix for non-fatal) - **Deps:** `engine/types.ts` (imports `RunState`, `ClaudeCliOutput` types for parsing). No runtime engine dependency — reads JSON files directly. ## 4. Data - **Entities:** - Handoff Artifact: Structured Markdown (01-spec.md through 07-changelog.md) - Agent Log: Claude CLI JSON output (`.sdlc/runs//logs/.json`) - Agent Prompt: SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (`.claude/skills/agent-/SKILL.md`) - Run State: JSON (`.sdlc/runs//state.json`) - Pipeline Config: YAML (`.sdlc/pipeline.yaml`). Top-level keys: `name`, `version`, `defaults`, `phases` (FR-33), `nodes`. `phases` key declares named phase groups with member stage IDs (e.g., `plan: [specification, design, decision]`). Engine treats `phases` as opaque config data. Node IDs use activity-based naming (FR-33): `specification`, `design`, `decision`, `implementation`, `build`, `verify`, `tech-lead-review`, `optimize` - ~~CommitResult~~: **Deleted** (FR-29: engine no longer commits) - ValidationRule: `{ type: "file_exists"|"file_not_empty"|"contains_section"| "custom_script"|"frontmatter_field", path?, field?, allowed?, ... }` - LoopResult: `{ ..., bodyResults: AgentResult[] }` — accumulated per-iteration agent results; consumed by `executeLoopNode()` callback for log saving - LoopNodeConfig: `{ ..., nodes: Record }` — inline body node definitions replacing `body: string[]`. Each key is a body node ID, value is its full node config. `condition_node` must reference a key in `nodes`. Body node ordering derived from `inputs` declarations via topo-sort (>1 entry requires at least one `inputs` reference to prevent disconnected graph with arbitrary order). - NodeState: `{ ..., cost_usd?: number }` — per-node cost from `ClaudeCliOutput.total_cost_usd`, set at completion via `markNodeCompleted()` optional param (FR-32) - RunState: `{ ..., total_cost_usd?: number }` — sum of all `nodes[*].cost_usd`, recomputed by `updateRunCost()` on each node completion (FR-32) - NodeConfig: `{ ..., run_on?: "always"|"success"|"failure", phase?: string, env?: Record, model?: string }` — `run_on` for conditional post-pipeline execution; `phase` for artifact directory grouping; `env` for node-level env vars; `model` for per-node Claude model override (FR-27) - **ERD:** N/A (file-based, no database). - **Migration:** N/A. ### 4.1 Inter-Node Data Flow - **Mechanism:** Filesystem-based. Each node reads input via `{{input.}}` template variable pointing to predecessor's output directory. No manifest. - **Directory structure:** `.sdlc/runs//[/]/` per node output. Phase subdir present when node's `phase` field is set in config. Example with phases: `.sdlc/runs/abc/plan/specification/`, `.sdlc/runs/abc/impl/build/`. Without phase: `.sdlc/runs/abc/some-node/` (backward-compatible flat layout). - **Validation:** Engine validates output via configurable rules (file_exists, file_not_empty, contains_section, custom_script, frontmatter_field) after each node. Validation failures trigger continuation (resume with error context) rather than immediate node failure. - `frontmatter_field`: Reads artifact file, extracts YAML frontmatter via `^---\n([\s\S]*?)\n---` regex, parses target field, checks value against allowed set. Config: `{ type: "frontmatter_field", path, field, allowed }`. - `contains_section`: Checks artifact file for presence of a markdown section. Supports `on_error: continue` (non-fatal). Used by meta-agent for "Fixes Applied" section validation. - Developer node uses `custom_script` validation rule (not `after` hook) for `deno task check`, enabling continuation-on-failure for check errors. - **Context management:** Claude CLI auto-compression handles large input sets. - **Template variables:** `{{node_dir}}`, `{{input.*}}`, `{{run_dir}}`, `{{run_id}}`, `{{args.*}}`, `{{env.*}}`, `{{loop.iteration}}`. - **After-hook conventions:** Commands run from repo root (no `cd {{run_dir}}` prefix needed). Use `|| true` suffix to prevent hook failure from killing the node. Example (sds-update diff capture): `git diff HEAD -- documents/design.md > {{node_dir}}/04a-sds-diff.md; [ -s {{node_dir}}/04a-sds-diff.md ] || echo "No changes" > {{node_dir}}/04a-sds-diff.md || true`. ### 4.2 Commit Strategy - **Branch:** Feature branch created by tech-lead agent (`git checkout -b sdlc/issue-`). Fallback for `--prompt` mode: `sdlc/{{run_id}}`. - **Commit cadence (FR-26):** Developer-owned commits. No dedicated committer agent nodes. Developer runs `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` after each task. Commit messages follow `sdlc(impl): ` format. - **PR creation:** Tech-lead creates draft PR (`gh pr create --draft`) before impl-loop. Developer pushes to same branch. QA posts PR review verdicts. - **Post-pipeline:** Tech-lead-review performs final review + CI gate + merge. - **Engine invariant:** Engine does NOT auto-commit (FR-14 preserved). All git operations happen inside agent prompts.

600→- **Failure behavior:** Failed nodes produce no commits. On_error: "fail" stops
   601→  pipeline; "continue" proceeds to next nodes. Each failed `NodeState` gets
   602→  `error_category?: ErrorCategory` — domain-agnostic enum:
   603→  `continuations_exhausted | timeout | cli_crash | hook_failure | hitl_timeout |
   604→  aborted | unknown`. Set by engine at failure point; downstream agents map
   605→  categories to domain actions.
   606→- **Resume:** `--resume <run-id>` skips completed nodes per state.json.
   607→
   608→## 5. Logic
   609→
   610→- **Algos:**
   611→  - **Continuation Loop**: invoke agent -> validate -> if fail: resume with
   612→    error context -> repeat (max N). If limit reached: fail node, trigger
   613→    Meta-Agent.
   614→  - **Developer+QA Loop**: Developer implements -> QA verifies -> if FAIL:
   615→    Developer reads QA report, fixes -> repeat (max 3). Body nodes defined
   616→    inline via loop's `nodes` sub-object (not top-level). Execution order
   617→    determined by topo-sort of body nodes' `inputs` declarations.
   618→  - **Secret Detection**: `gitleaks detect --no-git` runs as part of
   619→    `deno task check` (`scripts/check.ts`). `allowFailure=true` — skips if
   620→    gitleaks binary not found. Engine-level `safetyCheckDiff()` removed.
   621→  - **Verbose Output Flow** (`-v` mode, agent nodes only): In
   622→    `executeAgentNode()`: (1) resolve input artifact file paths+sizes from
   623→    `ctx.input` dirs via `Deno.stat()` → `verboseInputs()`, (2) `runAgent()`
   624→    (with `output` + `nodeId`) emits `verbosePrompt()` → Claude CLI executes →
   625→    `verboseValidation()` → on failure: `verboseContinuation()` → retry.
   626→    All verbose methods guarded by `verbosity !== "verbose"` — no-op in
   627→    default/quiet. Output: human-readable stderr lines with section headers.
   628→    Note: safety check and auto-commit verbose removed (engine no longer
   629→    performs these operations).
   630→  - **Loop Node Log Saving** (callback-based, no I/O in `loop.ts`):
   631→    `runLoop()` accumulates `AgentResult` per body-node iteration into
   632→    `LoopResult.bodyResults[]` (pure data, no filesystem ops). In
   633→    `executeLoopNode()` (`engine.ts`), the `onNodeComplete` callback iterates
   634→    `bodyResults`, calling `saveAgentLog()` with iteration-qualified nodeId
   635→    (`${id}-iter-${i}`). Guard: only on `result.success && result.output`.
   636→    `saveAgentLog()` errors caught and warned (non-fatal) — audit I/O must not
   637→    break loop execution. `runDir` resolved via `getRunDir(this.state.run_id)`
   638→    (already in engine scope).
   639→  - **Node Result Summary** (FR-30): After agent node completion, engine
   640→    displays one-line result summary via `OutputManager.nodeResult()`.
   641→    Two call sites: (1) `executeNode()` — after `markNodeCompleted()`, for
   642→    top-level agent nodes; `executeAgentNode()` returns `AgentResult | null`
   643→    (was `boolean`), `executeNode()` extracts `.output` field.
   644→    (2) `executeLoopNode()` `onNodeComplete` callback — calls `nodeResult()`
   645→    when `result.output` exists. Suppressed in quiet mode. Shown in default
   646→    and verbose modes.
   647→  - **Verbose Edge Cases** (behavioral contracts verified by tests):
   648→    - **Default mode (no `-v`):** All 4 verbose methods produce zero stderr
   649→      output. `OutputManager` constructed with `verbose=false` suppresses all
   650→      verbose calls unconditionally.
   651→    - **Empty input dir:** `resolveInputArtifacts()` returns empty list →
   652→      `verboseInputs()` reports `0 files` without error. No `Deno.stat()` calls.
   653→    - **Missing file stat:** `Deno.stat()` failure on input artifact →
   654→      graceful skip, verbose output includes error detail for affected path.
   655→  - **Phase Registry Init**: In `engine.ts` `run()`, `setPhaseRegistry(config)`
   656→    called before `ensureRunDirs()`. On `--resume`: config re-loaded from
   657→    `state.config_path`, then `setPhaseRegistry()` called (registry not persisted
   658→    in `state.json` — always rebuilt from config). `ensureRunDirs()` creates
   659→    phase subdirs (e.g., `plan/`, `impl/`, `report/`) when phases present.
   660→    Phase assignment (default pipeline, FR-26, FR-33):
   661→    - `plan`: specification, design, decision
   662→    - `impl`: implementation (body nodes `build`, `verify` defined inline via
   663→      `nodes` sub-object)
   664→    - `report`: optimize, tech-lead-review
   665→  - **Failure Hook Before Post-Pipeline Nodes (FR-34)**: When
   666→    `pipelineSuccess === false`, engine executes `config.defaults.on_failure_script`
   667→    (if configured) via `runFailureHook()` before post-pipeline nodes. Script
   668→    is pipeline-specific (e.g., `.sdlc/scripts/rollback-uncommitted.sh` performs
   669→    `git checkout -- . && git reset HEAD`). Engine treats it as opaque
   670→    `Deno.Command` invocation — domain-agnostic. Failed node IDs available via
   671→    `state.json` (`nodes[*].status === "failed"`) — no engine-written artifacts.
   672→  - **Post-Pipeline Node Collection & Ordering**: `collectPostPipelineNodes()`
   673→    collects nodes where `run_on !== undefined` (replaces `run_always`-based
   674→    collection). `sortPostPipelineNodes()` sorts them topologically using
   675→    `inputs` field (reuses `toposort()` from `dag.ts`).
   676→  - **Post-Pipeline Node Filtering**: Before executing each post-pipeline node,
   677→    engine applies per-node filter based on `run_on` value and
   678→    `pipelineSuccess`:
   679→    - `run_on: "always"` → execute unconditionally.
   680→    - `run_on: "success"` → skip if `!pipelineSuccess`, call
   681→      `markNodeSkipped()`.
   682→    - `run_on: "failure"` → skip if `pipelineSuccess`, call
   683→      `markNodeSkipped()`.
   684→  - **Meta-Agent Trigger**: Engine executes meta-agent via `run_on: "always"`.
   685→    After all DAG levels complete (success or failure), engine collects
   686→    post-pipeline nodes, sorts topologically, filters by condition (see above),
   687→    and executes in order. Meta-agent identifies failed nodes via `state.json`
   688→    (`nodes[*].status === "failed"`). Edits `.claude/skills/agent-*/SKILL.md` to fix diagnosed problems. Produces
   689→    minimal `07-changelog.md` listing applied fixes. Updates persistent memory
   690→    in `documents/meta.md`. Posts 2-3 line summary to GitHub issue.
   691→  - **Tech-Lead-Review Node**: Post-pipeline agent (`run_on: always`). Performs
   692→    final code review, checks CI gates, merges PR if all pass. Handles
   693→    missing-PR case gracefully (no-op with clear message when pipeline failed
   694→    before tech-lead created PR).
   695→  - **HITL via AskUserQuestion Interception** (FR-21):
   696→    Engine detects agent HITL requests by inspecting `permission_denials` in
   697→    Claude CLI JSON output. Flow:
   698→    1. Agent node completes → engine parses JSON `result` event.
   699→    2. If `permission_denials[]` contains entry with
   700→       `tool_name == "AskUserQuestion"`: extract `tool_input.questions` (structured
   701→       question with `question`, `header`, `options[]`, `multiSelect`) and
   702→       `session_id` from result.
   703→    3. Engine calls `defaults.hitl.ask_script` (external pipeline script) with
   704→       question JSON + context args (repo, issue, run-id, node-id).
   705→    4. Engine sets node state to `waiting` in `state.json`, saves `session_id`.
   706→    5. Engine enters poll loop: `sleep(poll_interval)` → call
   707→       `defaults.hitl.check_script` → if exit 0, read reply from stdout.
   708→    6. Engine resumes agent: `claude --resume <session_id> -p "<reply>"
   709→       --output-format json`. Agent sees full previous context + reply as new
   710→       user message.
   711→    7. On `timeout` exceeded: node marked `failed`, Meta-Agent triggered.
   712→    Experimentally verified (see `documents/rnd/human-in-the-loop.md`):
   713→    - `AskUserQuestion` denied in `-p` mode regardless of `--dangerously-skip-
   714→      permissions` (cause: no terminal, not permissions).
   715→    - Question JSON in `permission_denials[0].tool_input`: `{questions: [{
   716→      question, header, options: [{label, description}], multiSelect}]}`.
   717→    - `--resume <session_id> -p "<answer>"` preserves full session context;
   718→      agent correctly interprets answer in context of its original question.
   719→    - Cost per HITL roundtrip: ~$0.08 (question turn + resume turn).
   720→    Pipeline config:
   721→    ```yaml
   722→    defaults:
   723→      on_failure_script: .sdlc/scripts/rollback-uncommitted.sh
   724→      hitl:
   725→        ask_script: .sdlc/scripts/hitl-ask.sh
   726→        check_script: .sdlc/scripts/hitl-check.sh
   727→        artifact_source: plan/pm/01-spec.md
   728→        poll_interval: 60
   729→        timeout: 7200
   730→    ```
   731→- **Rules:**
   732→  - Artifacts overwritten on re-run (git history preserves previous).
   733→  - QA iteration numbering restarts on re-run.
   734→  - Meta-Agent runs on both success and failure.
   735→  - Meta-Agent auto-applies prompt improvements to `.claude/skills/agent-*/SKILL.md`.
   736→    Human review at PR merge via tech-lead-review.
   737→
   738→## 6. Non-Functional
   739→
   740→- **Scale:** Single pipeline per issue. Sequential stages (no parallel agents).
   741→- **Fault:** Stage failure stops pipeline, Meta-Agent analyzes, failure reported
   742→  on issue.
   743→- **Sec:** Secret detection via `gitleaks detect --no-git` in `deno task check`
   744→  (`scripts/check.ts`). Engine-level scope checks removed. Agents run with
   745→  local user's permissions.
   746→- **Logs:** Full transcripts per stage in `.sdlc/runs/<run-id>/logs/`.
   747→
   748→## 7. Constraints
   749→
   750→- **Simplified:** Pipeline runs sequentially (no parallel stages in v1).
   751→- **Deferred:** Multi-repo support. Parallel pipelines for multiple issues.
   752→  Issue size/complexity limits. Cost budget limits and alerts (per-node cost
   753→  aggregation implemented in FR-32; budget enforcement deferred).

   761→  `escHtml`). Tests in `scripts/generate-dashboard_test.ts`.

file.content:
- **Failure behavior:** Failed nodes produce no commits. On_error: "fail" stops
  pipeline; "continue" proceeds to next nodes. Each failed `NodeState` gets
  `error_category?: ErrorCategory` — domain-agnostic enum:
  `continuations_exhausted | timeout | cli_crash | hook_failure | hitl_timeout |
  aborted | unknown`. Set by engine at failure point; downstream agents map
  categories to domain actions.
- **Resume:** `--resume <run-id>` skips completed nodes per state.json.

## 5. Logic

- **Algos:**
  - **Continuation Loop**: invoke agent -> validate -> if fail: resume with
    error context -> repeat (max N). If limit reached: fail node, trigger
    Meta-Agent.
  - **Developer+QA Loop**: Developer implements -> QA verifies -> if FAIL:
    Developer reads QA report, fixes -> repeat (max 3). Body nodes defined
    inline via loop's `nodes` sub-object (not top-level). Execution order
    determined by topo-sort of body nodes' `inputs` declarations.
  - **Secret Detection**: `gitleaks detect --no-git` runs as part of
    `deno task check` (`scripts/check.ts`). `allowFailure=true` — skips if
    gitleaks binary not found. Engine-level `safetyCheckDiff()` removed.
  - **Verbose Output Flow** (`-v` mode, agent nodes only): In
    `executeAgentNode()`: (1) resolve input artifact file paths+sizes from
    `ctx.input` dirs via `Deno.stat()` → `verboseInputs()`, (2) `runAgent()`
    (with `output` + `nodeId`) emits `verbosePrompt()` → Claude CLI executes →
    `verboseValidation()` → on failure: `verboseContinuation()` → retry.
    All verbose methods guarded by `verbosity !== "verbose"` — no-op in
    default/quiet. Output: human-readable stderr lines with section headers.
    Note: safety check and auto-commit verbose removed (engine no longer
    performs these operations).
  - **Loop Node Log Saving** (callback-based, no I/O in `loop.ts`):
    `runLoop()` accumulates `AgentResult` per body-node iteration into
    `LoopResult.bodyResults[]` (pure data, no filesystem ops). In
    `executeLoopNode()` (`engine.ts`), the `onNodeComplete` callback iterates
    `bodyResults`, calling `saveAgentLog()` with iteration-qualified nodeId
    (`${id}-iter-${i}`). Guard: only on `result.success && result.output`.
    `saveAgentLog()` errors caught and warned (non-fatal) — audit I/O must not
    break loop execution. `runDir` resolved via `getRunDir(this.state.run_id)`
    (already in engine scope).
  - **Node Result Summary** (FR-30): After agent node completion, engine
    displays one-line result summary via `OutputManager.nodeResult()`.
    Two call sites: (1) `executeNode()` — after `markNodeCompleted()`, for
    top-level agent nodes; `executeAgentNode()` returns `AgentResult | null`
    (was `boolean`), `executeNode()` extracts `.output` field.
    (2) `executeLoopNode()` `onNodeComplete` callback — calls `nodeResult()`
    when `result.output` exists. Suppressed in quiet mode. Shown in default
    and verbose modes.
  - **Verbose Edge Cases** (behavioral contracts verified by tests):
    - **Default mode (no `-v`):** All 4 verbose methods produce zero stderr
      output. `OutputManager` constructed with `verbose=false` suppresses all
      verbose calls unconditionally.
    - **Empty input dir:** `resolveInputArtifacts()` returns empty list →
      `verboseInputs()` reports `0 files` without error. No `Deno.stat()` calls.
    - **Missing file stat:** `Deno.stat()` failure on input artifact →
      graceful skip, verbose output includes error detail for affected path.
  - **Phase Registry Init**: In `engine.ts` `run()`, `setPhaseRegistry(config)`
    called before `ensureRunDirs()`. On `--resume`: config re-loaded from
    `state.config_path`, then `setPhaseRegistry()` called (registry not persisted
    in `state.json` — always rebuilt from config). `ensureRunDirs()` creates
    phase subdirs (e.g., `plan/`, `impl/`, `report/`) when phases present.
    Phase assignment (default pipeline, FR-26, FR-33):
    - `plan`: specification, design, decision
    - `impl`: implementation (body nodes `build`, `verify` defined inline via
      `nodes` sub-object)
    - `report`: optimize, tech-lead-review
  - **Failure Hook Before Post-Pipeline Nodes (FR-34)**: When
    `pipelineSuccess === false`, engine executes `config.defaults.on_failure_script`
    (if configured) via `runFailureHook()` before post-pipeline nodes. Script
    is pipeline-specific (e.g., `.sdlc/scripts/rollback-uncommitted.sh` performs
    `git checkout -- . && git reset HEAD`). Engine treats it as opaque
    `Deno.Command` invocation — domain-agnostic. Failed node IDs available via
    `state.json` (`nodes[*].status === "failed"`) — no engine-written artifacts.
  - **Post-Pipeline Node Collection & Ordering**: `collectPostPipelineNodes()`
    collects nodes where `run_on !== undefined` (replaces `run_always`-based
    collection). `sortPostPipelineNodes()` sorts them topologically using
    `inputs` field (reuses `toposort()` from `dag.ts`).
  - **Post-Pipeline Node Filtering**: Before executing each post-pipeline node,
    engine applies per-node filter based on `run_on` value and
    `pipelineSuccess`:
    - `run_on: "always"` → execute unconditionally.
    - `run_on: "success"` → skip if `!pipelineSuccess`, call
      `markNodeSkipped()`.
    - `run_on: "failure"` → skip if `pipelineSuccess`, call
      `markNodeSkipped()`.
  - **Meta-Agent Trigger**: Engine executes meta-agent via `run_on: "always"`.
    After all DAG levels complete (success or failure), engine collects
    post-pipeline nodes, sorts topologically, filters by condition (see above),
    and executes in order. Meta-agent identifies failed nodes via `state.json`
    (`nodes[*].status === "failed"`). Edits `.claude/skills/agent-*/SKILL.md` to fix diagnosed problems. Produces
    minimal `07-changelog.md` listing applied fixes. Updates persistent memory
    in `documents/meta.md`. Posts 2-3 line summary to GitHub issue.
  - **Tech-Lead-Review Node**: Post-pipeline agent (`run_on: always`). Performs
    final code review, checks CI gates, merges PR if all pass. Handles
    missing-PR case gracefully (no-op with clear message when pipeline failed
    before tech-lead created PR).
  - **HITL via AskUserQuestion Interception** (FR-21):
    Engine detects agent HITL requests by inspecting `permission_denials` in
    Claude CLI JSON output. Flow:
    1. Agent node completes → engine parses JSON `result` event.
    2. If `permission_denials[]` contains entry with
       `tool_name == "AskUserQuestion"`: extract `tool_input.questions` (structured
       question with `question`, `header`, `options[]`, `multiSelect`) and
       `session_id` from result.
    3. Engine calls `defaults.hitl.ask_script` (external pipeline script) with
       question JSON + context args (repo, issue, run-id, node-id).
    4. Engine sets node state to `waiting` in `state.json`, saves `session_id`.
    5. Engine enters poll loop: `sleep(poll_interval)` → call
       `defaults.hitl.check_script` → if exit 0, read reply from stdout.
    6. Engine resumes agent: `claude --resume <session_id> -p "<reply>"
       --output-format json`. Agent sees full previous context + reply as new
       user message.
    7. On `timeout` exceeded: node marked `failed`, Meta-Agent triggered.
    Experimentally verified (see `documents/rnd/human-in-the-loop.md`):
    - `AskUserQuestion` denied in `-p` mode regardless of `--dangerously-skip-
      permissions` (cause: no terminal, not permissions).
    - Question JSON in `permission_denials[0].tool_input`: `{questions: [{
      question, header, options: [{label, description}], multiSelect}]}`.
    - `--resume <session_id> -p "<answer>"` preserves full session context;
      agent correctly interprets answer in context of its original question.
    - Cost per HITL roundtrip: ~$0.08 (question turn + resume turn).
    Pipeline config:
    ```yaml
    defaults:
      on_failure_script: .sdlc/scripts/rollback-uncommitted.sh
      hitl:
        ask_script: .sdlc/scripts/hitl-ask.sh
        check_script: .sdlc/scripts/hitl-check.sh
        artifact_source: plan/pm/01-spec.md
        poll_interval: 60
        timeout: 7200
    ```
- **Rules:**
  - Artifacts overwritten on re-run (git history preserves previous).
  - QA iteration numbering restarts on re-run.
  - Meta-Agent runs on both success and failure.
  - Meta-Agent auto-applies prompt improvements to `.claude/skills/agent-*/SKILL.md`.
    Human review at PR merge via tech-lead-review.

## 6. Non-Functional

- **Scale:** Single pipeline per issue. Sequential stages (no parallel agents).
- **Fault:** Stage failure stops pipeline, Meta-Agent analyzes, failure reported
  on issue.
- **Sec:** Secret detection via `gitleaks detect --no-git` in `deno task check`
  (`scripts/check.ts`). Engine-level scope checks removed. Agents run with
  local user's permissions.
- **Logs:** Full transcripts per stage in `.sdlc/runs/<run-id>/logs/`.

## 7. Constraints

- **Simplified:** Pipeline runs sequentially (no parallel stages in v1).
- **Deferred:** Multi-repo support. Parallel pipelines for multiple issues.
  Issue size/complexity limits. Cost budget limits and alerts (per-node cost
  aggregation implemented in FR-32; budget enforcement deferred).

## 8. SRS Evidence Status

All FR evidence for issue #15 is complete:

- **FR-35 (Dashboard Result Summary Display):** Implemented. SRS section 3.34
  evidence recorded — `scripts/generate-dashboard.ts` (`renderCard`,
  `escHtml`). Tests in `scripts/generate-dashboard_test.ts`.
- **FR-38 (Timeline Visualization):** Implemented. SRS section 3.37 evidence
  recorded — `scripts/generate-dashboard.ts` (`computeTimeline`,
  `renderTimeline`, `.timeline-bottleneck` CSS). Tests in
  `scripts/generate-dashboard_test.ts`. Evidence committed in `e493cbb`.
- **FR-39 (Repeated File Read Warning):** Implemented. SRS section 3.38
  evidence recorded — `engine/agent.ts` (`FileReadTracker` class). Tests in
  `engine/agent_test.ts`. Evidence committed in `e493cbb`.
- **FR-40 (Dashboard Stream Log Links):** Implemented. SRS section 3.39
  evidence recorded — `scripts/generate-dashboard.ts` (`streamLogHref`,
  `.log-link` CSS). Tests in `scripts/generate-dashboard_test.ts`.
- **FR-42 (Agent Output Summary):** Already implemented. All 7 agent SKILL.md
  files document `## Summary` in output format. `pipeline.yaml` enforces
  `contains_section: Summary` on 6 agent nodes (`specification`, `design`,
  `decision`, `verify`, `optimize`, `tech-lead-review`); Developer (`build`)
  enforced via `custom_script: deno task check`. Evidence:
  `.claude/skills/agent-*/SKILL.md` (7 files), `.sdlc/pipeline.yaml` (7 rules).

``` ### 2.3 Pipeline DAG (FR-26, FR-33) ```mermaid graph LR Spec["specification"] --> Design["design
(solution plan)"] Design --> Decision["decision
(critique+branch+PR)"] Decision --> Loop["implementation
(build→verify)"] Loop -.-> Review["tech-lead-review
(run_on:always)"] Loop -.-> Optimize["optimize
(run_on:always)"] ``` - **Node ID convention (FR-33):** Activity-based IDs reflect what work is done, not who does it. Mapping: `pm`→`specification`, `architect`→`design`, `tech-lead`→`decision`, `impl-loop`→`implementation`, `developer`→`build`, `qa`→`verify`, `tech-lead-review`→`tech-lead-review`, `meta-agent`→`optimize`. - **Phases (FR-33):** Top-level `phases:` key in `pipeline.yaml` declares named phase groups. Each phase lists member stage IDs: - `plan`: [specification, design, decision] - `impl`: [implementation] - `report`: [tech-lead-review, optimize] Phase grouping is declarative config; engine treats it as opaque data. Enables future phase-level `run_on` semantics and cleaner artifact reporting. - **Subsystems:** - **Pipeline Engine** (`engine/`): Deno/TypeScript DAG-based executor with YAML config, template interpolation, parallel levels, loop nodes, human nodes, resume support - **Agent Runtime**: Claude Code CLI invocations with role-specific prompts from `.claude/skills/agent-/SKILL.md` (canonical, agentskills.io- compliant; no symlinks) - **Artifact Store**: Git-tracked files in `.sdlc/runs//[/]/` (phase subdir present when node has `phase` field in config) - **Validation Engine**: Rule-based checks (file_exists, file_not_empty, contains_section, custom_script, frontmatter_field) - **Continuation Engine**: `--resume` based re-invocation on validation failure or safety-check violation (shared `max_continuations` budget) - **Legacy Shell Scripts** (`.sdlc/scripts/`): Preserved for backward compatibility, superseded by engine ## 3. Components ### 3.1 Docker Image - **Purpose:** Single runtime environment for all stages. - **Interfaces:** Contains `claude` CLI, `deno`, `git`, `gh`, `gitleaks`. - **Deps:** Node.js (for claude CLI install), Deno runtime. ### 3.2 Stage Scripts (`.sdlc/scripts/`) — DEPRECATED - **Status:** Formally deprecated. Superseded by Deno/TypeScript pipeline engine (`engine/`). Retained for backward compatibility only. Use `deno task run`. - **Purpose:** Legacy orchestration for each pipeline stage: prepare input, invoke agent, validate, continue, commit. - **AGENT_PROMPT paths:** Updated to `.claude/skills/agent-/SKILL.md` (canonical, post-FR-36 migration). - **Interfaces:** - Input: `` as CLI argument. - Output: Committed artifacts + logs on feature branch. - **Deps:** `lib.sh` (shared functions), `claude` CLI, `git`, `gh`. ### 3.3 Shared Library (`.sdlc/scripts/lib.sh`) - **Purpose:** Common functions for all stage scripts. - **Interfaces:** Functions: `log()`, `run_agent()`, `validate_artifact()`, `continuation_loop()`, `commit_artifacts()`, `report_status()`, `safety_check_diff()`, `retry_with_backoff()`. - `retry_with_backoff()`: Generic retry wrapper for external CLI calls (`claude`, `gh`). Max 3 attempts, 5s initial delay, 2x backoff. Retries on non-zero exit (network/rate-limit errors). Does not retry validation failures. - **Deps:** `claude` CLI, `git`, `gh`. ### 3.4 Agent Skills (`.claude/skills/agent-*`) (FR-36) - **Purpose:** Versioned system prompts defining each agent's role and behavior. Each agent lives in `.claude/skills/agent-/SKILL.md` (canonical, agentskills.io-compliant). Dual-use: pipeline-driven (via engine `prompt:` config) and interactive (via Claude Code `/agent-` slash commands). - **Directory structure:** `.claude/skills/agent-/SKILL.md` — 7 agents: - `agent-pm` — triages open GitHub issues, selects highest-priority, produces spec. - `agent-architect` — design-solution role: produces implementation plan with 2-3 variants, affected files, effort estimates, risk analysis. - `agent-tech-lead` — critique + decision + SDS update + branch creation (`git checkout -b sdlc/issue-`) + draft PR (`gh pr create --draft`) + task breakdown from selected variant. Uses `{{run_id}}` for `--prompt` mode fallback branch `sdlc/{{run_id}}`. - `agent-developer` — implements tasks. Owns `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` after each task. Posts PR comment with implementation summary. - `agent-qa` — verifies developer output. Posts verdict as PR review (`gh pr review`: approve/request-changes). - `agent-tech-lead-review` — post-pipeline: final code review + CI gate check + merge. `run_on: always`. Handles missing-PR case gracefully. - `agent-meta-agent` — prompt optimization, failure analysis. - **Removed agents (FR-26):** `tech-lead-reviewer`, `tech-lead-sds`, `committer`, `code-reviewer`. - **SKILL.md frontmatter (agentskills.io-compliant):** ```yaml --- name: "agent-" description: "" compatibility: ["claude-code"] allowed-tools: [] --- ``` - `compatibility: ["claude-code"]` — declares runtime compatibility. - `allowed-tools: []` — no automatic tool grants; agents use tools available in their execution context. - **Interfaces:** - Pipeline: engine reads `prompt:` path from `pipeline.yaml` (now `.claude/skills/agent-/SKILL.md`), caches file content at config load time (`prompt_content`), passes inline via `claude --append-system-prompt`. Fallback to `--append-system-prompt-file` for template paths. - Interactive: Claude Code discovers skills directly from `.claude/skills/agent-/SKILL.md` → user invokes `/agent-`. No symlinks required (canonical location). - **Agent Execution Summary (FR-40, FR-42):** All 7 agents must produce a `## Summary` section in their output artifacts. Content: 2-5 bullet points (actions taken, key decisions, artifacts produced, issues encountered). 6 agents (PM, Architect, Tech Lead, QA, Meta-Agent, Tech Lead Review) append `## Summary` to their markdown artifact files. Developer includes summary in commit message body (no separate artifact file). Pipeline enforces via `contains_section: Summary` validation on 6 nodes (`specification`, `design`, `decision`, `verify`, `optimize`, `tech-lead-review`). Developer (`build`) excluded from file-based validation — uses existing `custom_script: deno task check`. - **Voice Convention (FR-40):** Each SKILL.md contains a `## Voice` section (after `# Role:` heading, before `## Responsibilities`) mandating first-person narrative ("I") in all agent outputs (GitHub comments, artifacts, reports). Passive/third-person prohibited in narrative text. YAML frontmatter and code blocks excluded. Each agent's section includes 2-3 role-specific correct vs incorrect examples anchored to that agent's typical output types (e.g., PM: "I selected issue #42" not "Issue #42 was selected"; QA: "I verified all criteria" not "All criteria were verified"). - **Migration (FR-36):** Complete. Formerly `agents//SKILL.md` with symlinks from `.claude/skills/`. Migrated to canonical `.claude/skills/` layout; `agents/` directory removed; symlink indirection eliminated.
